verifiedGood: Parser + normalizer are stable on the currently known ingredient set; no parser roundtrip issues were found.
Core audit modules loaded successfully from the current codebase.
The audit runner is read-only with respect to engine logic; it only writes audit reports.
Canonical ingredient truth can be measured from the current code because ingredient registries are separately declared in the codebase.
Pet-toxin behavior can be exercised directly through analyzeProduct() using a pet profile.
verifiedRisky: Some pet-toxin ingredients are present in the codebase but do not consistently surface both a pet warning flag and a petToxins hit.
Many normalized ingredients exist in only one registry, which means your truth model is fragmented.
Some ingredients appear to be penalized through multiple channels, creating real overlap-risk candidates for double-counting.
Some overlap checks are inconclusive because not all engines expose the same level of diagnostic detail.
unverifiedAssumptions: This audit does not prove medical correctness of ingredient risk values; it only audits internal code consistency.
This audit does not prove any guessed synonym list for pet toxins; it only reports misses against your current code.
If graphRiskEngine/contextRiskEngine are incomplete or intentionally overlapping, overlap findings indicate risk, not final proof of a bug.
This audit does not benchmark real device performance or camera-thread smoothness.
This audit does not validate Open Food Facts payload quality or barcode coverage.
immediateFixes: Create one canonical ingredient source model, then derive INFO, GRAPH, RULES, and KNOWLEDGE views from it.
Unify pet-toxin truth so the same canonical source drives both petToxins detection and user-facing toxic-for-pets flags.
Review every HIGH overlap item and decide whether graph/context/base penalties should stack or de-duplicate.
Backfill the most important high-risk and condition-sensitive ingredients first instead of auto-filling everything with generic graph nodes.
Add explicit audit tests for diabetic, pet, and neutral profiles on the top risky ingredients.
safeLaterUpgrades: Phase 2: add a local SQLite product cache layer for high-frequency barcodes before network fallback.
Phase 2: store canonical normalized ingredients and precomputed summaries alongside cached barcode data.
Phase 2: add confidence metadata so the UI can distinguish verified analysis from partial analysis.
Phase 3: add snapshot/visual regression after engine truth and cache behavior are stable.
Phase 3: add premium motion and visual polish only after scoring integrity and safety coverage are hardened.

finalFlags: Unhealthy ingredient: high fructose corn syrup
